data.title: NanoViricides announces $3.8M registered direct offering; shares down
data.description:
• NanoViricides (NNVC) entered into a securities purchase agreement with a single institutional investor for a registered direct offering.
  • The offering includes 2.52M common shares (or pre-funded warrants) and accompanying warrants to purchase an additional 2.52M shares.
  • The transaction is expected to generate approximately $3.8M in gross proceeds before fees and expenses.
  • Each warrant has an exercise price of $1.75 per share and a 5.5-year term.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about July 27, 2026.
  • NNVC shares down 11% premarket.

data.title: Intel rises after Q2 beat; analysts largely positive but mull valuation, Foundry business
data.description:
Shares of Intel (INTC) rose about 3% premarket on Friday after second-quarter results and guidance beat estimates.

However, Seeking Alpha analysts highlighted low expectations making the "print look like a home run," and the Foundry segment, which "is still not really a well-working business." Meanwhile, Wall Street analysts were largely positive about the company but highlighted "rich" valuation and thoughts around data center CPU share.

SA Analysts

"Back in early June, I downgraded Intel (INTC) to a sell following Nvidia (NVDA) penetrating the consumer PC processor market with the RTX Spark superchip. Part of what justified my sell at the time was that the stock was overvalued. What I’ve seen since then is the stock reset, dropping about 26% over the past month alone," said Seeking Alpha analyst The Techie. "I think this helped create a setup with low enough expectations that is making this print look like a home run. The results are good, but I’ve seen better results get punished this quarter. I think the market was looking for a positive to price in here, as the guide is better than expected, though the company still printed a sequential decline and soft margin expansion."

For the period ending June 27, Intel earned an adjusted $0.42 per share as revenue jumped 25% year-over-year to come in at $16.13B. The company expects third-quarter revenue to be between $15.8B and $16.8B, with the midpoint of $16.3B well above the $15.06B analysts were anticipating. 

"Intel has just posted a relatively strong quarter with revenue coming in at $16.1B, up 25% Y/Y and roughly above 12% of the consensus with non-GAAP EPS of 42 cents nearly doubling the consensus thanks to a surge of 59% in DCAI [Data Center and AI Group]," said Seeking Alpha analyst Louis Gerard.

However, the analyst noted that "the Foundry segment is still not really a well-working business as, yes it posted a revenue base of $5.77, which is up 31% Y/Y but it still posted an operating loss of -$2.09B and with the intersegment eliminations were set to -$5.48B so external foundry revenue only stood at a mere $300MM. And to be fair, the whole bull thesis behind INTC is about 18A external traction which has not been reflected in the earnings."

Gerard added that the $11B GAAP loss, which is not an impairment but an escrowed-shares derivative as the bridge is a $12.5B mark-to-market charge on shares held in escrow for the Commerce Department under the CHIPS Secure Enclave arrangement.

"Though it does not impact the overall operation it is worth noting that the higher INTC trades, the large the charge will be as the government’s claim appreciates with the equity," Gerard noted.

Wall Street Analysts

BofA reiterated its Buy rating and $160 price objective on Intel's stock.

"Intel’s (INTC) Q2 beat/raise earnings call reconfirms the two pillars of our Buy thesis: 1) Equity External manufacturing (foundry) customer discussions have reached a more tangible stage, leading to capex boost (positive semicaps), and 2) INTC's core server CPU business is strongly participating in the ongoing agentic cycle, with data center sales surging 59% YoY, INTC's best growth rate in the past 15 years. We reiterate Buy and continue to view INTC's US-based leading-edge capacity and strong backing by the White House as long-term competitive moats," said analysts led by Vivek Arya.

The analysts noted that risk-wise we must watch for any capital market action to fund the capex, though they noted that Intel has other levers, like non-core asset disposals, plus potential customer prepayments as alternatives.

Arya and his team added that external foundry customers will start their engagements this year and into 2027, with larger revenues more in 2028-30, leading to a few years of high capex and low free cash flow, or FCF.

Needham kept its Hold rating on Intel's stock with no price target.

"Intel reported a beat and raise, as all segments exceeded expectations, led by DCAI despite tight supply," said analysts led by N. Quinn Bolton.

However, the analysts noted that "despite the turnaround underway, we remain Hold rated as we believe Intel is losing share in DC [data center] CPUs, the company is not competitive in Al accelerators and valuation is rich."

Wedbush kept its Neutral rating but increased the price target to $98 from $60.

"Intel handily exceeded prior guidance. And the company's new forecast, despite outpacing prior consensus, also looks extremely beatable given strong underlying conditions (and in particular higher CPU PX). Net, we expect fundamentals to remain positive for the world's largest producer of compute. At the same time, we still struggle to justify Intel's valuation (particularly relative to its peers) and thus remain on the sidelines with regard to the stock," said analyst Matt Bryson.

For AMD (AMD), Intel's results were a positive. Bryson said they expect AMD to be able to better take advantage of lifting average selling prices, or ASPs, and a shift towards higher-margin server products, with the analyst's conversations indicating that over the course of the second quarter, AMD has outperformed Intel in supplying incremental product.

For Memory makers the results were positive too. The analyst noted that more server CPUs require more Registered Dual In-Line Memory Modules, or RDIMMs, and more storage, among other things.

In addition, the results were positive for semiconductor capital equipment makers. Bryson said that Intel, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), and Micron Technology (MU) have now all increased spending plans.

Top chip equipment makers include ASML (ASML), Applied Materials (AMAT), Lam Research (LRCX), and KLA (KLAC).

Update: The story was updated with additional analysts' commentary and stock price movement.

data.title: Zevra plunges on EU snub for rare disease therapy
data.description:
• Zevra Therapeutics (ZVRA) lost ~30% in the premarket on Friday after an expert panel of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency, declined to endorse its lead product, arimoclomol, for Niemann-Pick disease type C, a rare neurodegenerative condition.
  • The EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use issued a negative opinion in connection with the company’s Marketing Authorization Application for arimoclomol in NPC, Boston, Massachusetts-based Zevra (ZVRA) said.
  • The company stated it will seek a reexamination of CHMP’s decision, which effectively shuts arimoclomol’s market entry in Europe while the product is already available in the U.S. for NPC.
  • “While the review did not result in the outcome we had hoped for, we are committed to investigating appropriate pathways to bring arimoclomol to patients in Europe,” CEO Neil McFarlane added.

data.title: AbbVie, Genmab announce late-stage trial setback for lymphoma therapy
data.description:
AbbVie (ABBV) and Genmab (GMAB) announced that their jointly developed bispecific antibody epcoritamab, marketed as Epkinly in the U.S., failed to meet the primary endpoint in a late-stage trial for patients with diffuse large B-cell lymphoma.

Citing topline data from their Phase 3 EPCORE DLBCL-1 study, the companies announced in January that epcoritamab didn’t lead to a statistically significant improvement in overall survival, the gold standard for measuring the efficacy of a cancer therapy.

The global trial was designed to have different primary endpoints by region, and overall survival was the sole primary endpoint in the U.S., AbbVie (ABBV) and Genmab (GMAB) clarified in a statement late Thursday.

Epcoritamab is currently available in the U.S. under the FDA's accelerated approval pathway for adults with relapsed or refractory DLBCL. The ongoing EPCORE DLBCL-1 trial is evaluating it against the investigator's choice of chemotherapy in adults with R/R DLBCL for whom high-dose chemotherapy and autologous stem cell transplant are not indicated.

data.title: Lufax announces board changes, CFO resignation
data.description:
• Lufax (LU) announced board and senior management changes effective July 25, 2026.
  • Fangfang Cai, Shibang Guo, and Peifeng Li resigned as non-executive directors and stepped down from their board committee roles.
  • Tongzhuan Xi resigned as executive director, chief financial officer, and Hong Kong authorised representative.
  • The internal team will temporarily oversee CFO responsibilities during the transition.
  • CEO Xiang Ji has been appointed as the company's Hong Kong authorised representative, replacing Tongzhuan Xi.
  • Wai Kin Chim was appointed as an independent non-executive director for an initial three-year term, effective July 25, 2026.

data.title: Mobix Labs to acquire Vision Aerial for $15M
data.description:
• Mobix Labs (MOBX) has agreed to acquire Vision Aerial, a U.S.-based designer and manufacturer of unmanned aerial systems, for approximately $15M, payable in a combination of cash and Mobix Labs common stock.
  • The transaction is expected to close this quarter, subject to customary closing conditions, including shareholder approval.
  • Upon closing, Mobix Labs expects to integrate Vision Aerial into its expanding autonomous systems business while retaining Vision Aerial’s key leadership and U.S.-based manufacturing operations.
  • The deal accelerates Mobix Labs’ global aerial intelligence strategy and National Security Matters Initiative.
  • MOBX shares rose over 8% premarket to $2.07.
